claim_text: The TWIS article argues that Western capitalism helped build the cheap China production system and that later political efforts to restrict or reprice cheap direct buying risk pushing costs onto poorer consumers.

EU finance ministers agreed to set a temporary 3 euro customs duty on low-value e-commerce parcels entering the bloc from July 1, 2026, while working toward removal of the customs-duty exemption for online purchases below 150 euros.
claimant: Reuters report citing the Council of the EU

claim_text: The European Commission said around 4.6 billion low-value consignments with a value not exceeding 150 euros entered the EU market in 2024, and linked this surge to customs reform, market fairness, compliance pressure, and environmental concerns.

IFS research reported by the Guardian found that poorer UK households were hit harder by cheapflation because cheaper grocery products rose faster than more expensive varieties, increasing the food-cost burden on lower-income consumers.
claimant: Guardian report on Institute for Fiscal Studies research

claim_text: Reuters reported that Shein and Temu increasingly appeal to shoppers earning less than 50000 dollars per year, and that more low-income shoppers are buying online to find bargains.
claimant: Reuters report citing Bank of America credit card data
